questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
options: Neither he nor his brother were going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother are going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother have been going to the party.
Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.
questions.solution: Neither he nor his brother is going to the party.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The book is superior ____ the other one.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: One of the miners have been hospitalised.
options: One of the miners has been hospitalised.
One of the miners is being hospitalised.
One of the miners are hospitalised.
One of the miners were hospitalised.
questions.solution: One of the miners has been hospitalised.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: ____ the circumstances, we decided to postpone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The team is working on the project since May.
options: The team had been working on the project since May.
The team was working on the project since May.
The team has worked on the project since May.
The team has been working on the project since May.
questions.solution: The team has been working on the project since May.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government appealed ____ the public to stay calm.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She lost not only her wallet but also.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The road is lined ____ trees on both sides.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is better than any student in the class.
options: He is best than any student in the class.
He is better than any other student in the class.
He is the best than any student in the class.
He is better than any student in the class.
questions.solution: He is better than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The teacher was satisfied ____ the results.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The price of these goods have increased.
options: The price of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods are increased.
The prices of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods have increased.
questions.solution: The price of these goods has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The patient died ____ a heart attack.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: No sooner he arrived than the meeting started.
options: No sooner did he arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he arrives than the meeting started.
No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
questions.solution: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is interested ____ pursuing higher studies.

questions.stem: Correct: Neither the manager nor his assistants was present.
options: Neither the manager nor his assistants are being present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants has been present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants is present.
questions.solution: Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: It is high time we leave for the station.
options: It is high time we leave for the station.
It is high time we have left for the station.
It is high time we were leaving for the station.
It is high time we left for the station.
questions.solution: It is high time we left for the station.

questions.stem: Antonym of AMICABLE:

options: A number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents has increased.
The number of accidents is increasing.
The number of accidents have increased.
questions.solution: The number of accidents has increased.

questions.stem: Antonym of TENACIOUS:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'maximum demand' charge in a two-part tariff is based on:
options: The peak kVA/kW the consumer draws
questions.solution: A two-part tariff has a fixed charge tied to maximum demand plus an energy charge per kWh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RLC at f=135Hz (above fr=112.50Hz). Circuit behavior:
questions.solution: At f>fr, XL > XC. Phase angle positive. Power factor lagging.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Motor action:
options: Force on current-carrying conductor in magnetic field F = BIl
questions.solution: F = BIl sinθ. Fleming's left-hand rule.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 10 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 10×40/50 = 8 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF of a single lead-acid cell is about:
questions.solution: A lead-acid cell gives ~2 V; a 12 V battery uses six cells in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RLC at f=352.30Hz (below fr=355.90Hz), R=5Ω. Circuit behavior:
questions.solution: At f<fr, XC > XL. Phase angle negative. Power factor leading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Faraday's law:
options: EMF = -dφ/dt. Induced voltage proportional to rate of change of flux linkage
questions.solution: Faraday: e = -N·dφ/dt. Transformer EMF vs Motional EMF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bundled conductors:
options: Reduce corona, increase GMR, reduce line reactance
questions.solution: Bundling: 2, 3, or 4 sub-conductors per phase. EHV transmission lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 220 V draws 20 A with armature resistance 0.8 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A wattmeter reads zero on a purely reactive (inductive or capacitive) load because:
options: The average of v·i over a cycle is zero
questions.solution: Pure reactance gives a 90° phase shift; instantaneous power averages to zero over a cycle, so real power is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Copper losses: I²R in windings
questions.solution: Cu loss ∝ I² (variable). Max efficiency when Cu loss = Core loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parallel operation conditions:
options: Same V ratio, same %Z, same polarity, same phase shift
questions.solution: Mismatched %Z: lower Z takes more load. Same vector group essential.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: All-day efficiency:
questions.solution: Important for distribution transformers (variable load). Annual η uses 8760h.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Switching (power electronic) devices:
questions.solution: SCR (thyristor): latching, turns off at zero current. MOSFET: fast switching. IGBT: high power, high speed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona:
options: Ionization of air around conductor when E > critical. Causes power loss, noise, O₃
questions.solution: Corona loss: P_c ∝ f·(V-V_c)². Reduced by using bundled conductors, larger diameter.

questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 30 kW and slip 0.05. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.05 × 30 kW = 1500 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.2 H, C = 100 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.2×100µF)) ≈ 35.6 Hz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing series and parallel RLC resonance, which statement is correct?
options: Series resonance gives minimum impedance; parallel (anti-)resonance gives maximum impedance.
questions.solution: Series resonance → minimum Z (max current); ideal parallel resonance → maximum Z (min line current).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 100 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×100µF)) ≈ 50.3 Hz.
